The wi_skills robots.txt fix is cleaner (removes the Disallow entirely) while wo_skills adds 'Allow: /collections/' which is functionally equivalent but less conventional. Overall, wi_skills is more efficient and produces a cleaner output, while wo_skills demonstrates comparable depth through more extensive data analysis. Quality is roughly equivalent, with wi_skills having a slight edge in execution efficiency and output cleanliness.

utility_details.judge_error: No critical errors detected in either run. Both agents encountered 'jq: command not found' when attempting jq validation, but successfully fell back to Node.js for JSON parsing. Both output files were produced and passed all verifier checks.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills achieved passing scores across all seven evaluation items and passed all seven verifier checks. Both produced complete on-page optimization packages and valid schema markup files covering all eight pages. However, wi_skills demonstrates higher quality in several dimensions: (1) it produced a larger, more detailed document (26,581 bytes vs 17,228 bytes); (2) the schema file is more comprehensive with FAQPage schema and richer metadata alongside Product and BreadcrumbList; (3) URL mapping is presented as a full table with absolute canonical URLs rather than relative paths; (4) the overall structure follows SEO skill guidelines more precisely (e.g., explicit character count awareness, more granular keyword strategy, sitewide implementation notes aligned with skill guidance). The wo_skills run is competent and production-ready but less thorough and slightly less implementation-precise. Overall, wi_skills produces a higher-quality deliverable.
